Project 01 · Academic · SolidWorks
NC Drilling Machine
// Photo Gallery
Overview

Manual drilling machines require constant operator involvement and lack precision for repeated tasks. We designed an NC Drilling Machine on a 3-axis system — similar to a 3D printer — to automate drilling of soft materials like wood.

My Role

Responsible for designing the machine axes and overall orientation. Designed the motion system using stepper motors and lead screws, and created 3D printed motor mounts. Collaborated with the electrical team for Arduino-based motor control.

Key Challenge

Vibration during drilling caused inaccurate positioning. Solved by adding chromium smooth rods alongside each lead screw to stabilise the moving block.

Process
  • Designed 3-axis lead screw + smooth rod system in SolidWorks
  • 3D printed custom motor mounts and carriage blocks
  • Integrated NEMA stepper motors with Arduino
  • FEA stress & displacement analysis on critical parts
  • Motion study simulation to validate movements
Tools
SolidWorksANSYS3D PrintingArduinoNEMA 17Lead ScrewsChromium Rods
Motion Study
Project 02 · Internship · AutoCAD
Lamborghini Countach Replica
Overview

Final project for the ACMEGRADE AutoCAD internship — recreating the iconic Lamborghini Countach as a detailed 2D technical drawing with side, top, and rear orthographic views with full colour and layering.

My Role

The reference drawing was provided. My task was to replicate it accurately in AutoCAD — all dimensions had to be interpreted and adjusted by me from proportional analysis and automotive knowledge.

Process
  • Studied reference views and planned layer structure
  • Built geometry using polylines, arcs and splines
  • Self-determined all dimensions from proportional analysis
  • Applied colour hatching for body, glass and chassis
  • Annotated with dimensions and technical notes
Tools
AutoCAD 20242D DraftingHatching & LayersDimensioning
Outcome

Earned both Trainee Completion and Internship Completion certificates from ACMEGRADE.

Project 03 · Final Year · In Progress
Soft Robotic Glove
Overview

A lightweight soft robotic glove for hand rehabilitation and assisted grip. Uses soft pneumatic actuators that mimic natural finger bending — safer, lighter and more comfortable than rigid exoskeletons.

My Role

Responsible for design and FEA analysis of soft pneumatic actuators — modelled as multi-body parts to simulate bending motion and determine internal pressure requirements. Also designing all 3D printed connecting parts, finger joints and housing box.

Key Challenge

Designing soft actuators that produce sufficient bending force while remaining flexible, lightweight and manufacturable via FDM.

Process
  • Designed actuator geometry as multi-body SolidWorks part
  • FEA to simulate bending motion under pneumatic pressure
  • Determined required internal pressure for full finger flexion
  • Designing 3D printable finger joints and housing components
Tools
SolidWorksANSYS3D PrintingSoft RoboticsPneumatic ActuationFEA
Status

In active development — actuator design and analysis underway. Prototype photos coming soon.

// About Me

The Person Behind the Projects

I'm a Mechanical Engineering undergraduate at DJSCE, Mumbai, specialising with Honors in Electric Vehicles. My passion lies in translating complex engineering problems into elegant, manufacturable designs.

With over 120 hours of professional CAD training at CADD Centre, I've developed deep proficiency in SolidWorks, CATIA, and ANSYS Workbench — spanning 3D modelling to advanced FEA simulation.

I'm currently developing a Soft Robotic Glove for hand rehabilitation — leading the design and FEA of soft pneumatic actuators and all 3D printed components.

"Eager to apply technical skills and creativity in designing functional, aesthetically appealing, and manufacturable products."

Based in Mumbai, Maharashtra — open to internships, full-time roles, and collaborative projects in product design, mechanical design, or EV systems.

Current Focus
Soft Robotic Glove — FEA, soft actuator design & 3D printed components
Specialisation
B.Tech Mechanical Engineering with Honours in Electric Vehicles · CGPA 8.44
CAD Expertise
SolidWorks · CATIA V5 · AutoCAD · Autodesk Inventor · ANSYS Workbench
